body.title: [sprint] Route local tiers to Ollama on Hex — per-tier ollama ModelSpecs + rules, local-first cascade
body.intention: I intend that DickSimnel's builder inference runs on free local Hex, so the DS cost constraint lifts and the first real build is affordable.
body.description:
**Problem:** DS 'ollama-only mode' requests model `devstral-small-2:24b`, but EVERY Ollama ModelSpec in the registry is bound to source `ollama_cloud` — the local `ollama` source (sources.py:254, base_url from `INFERENCE_ENDPOINT`, default 127.0.0.1:11434) has ZERO models registered. So a worker dispatch resolves to the CLOUD, never to Hex. Pointing `INFERENCE_ENDPOINT` at Hex alone does nothing because the rules engine has no local-ollama candidate to pick.
**CONFIRMED HEX ROSTER (probed/pulled 2026-06-30, sized to 32GB unified memory):** minion=`llama3.2:3b` (2GB), worker=`devstral-small-2:24b` (15GB, agentic) + `qwen2.5-coder:14b` (9GB, coder fallback), analyst=`deepseek-r1:14b` (9GB, reasoner). designer stays Claude-direct (cloud, by design). The registry's cloud Ollama tags do NOT transfer locally: `qwen3-coder-next` is 48GB (won't fit) and `deepseek-v4-flash` 404s as a local tag — these stay on the flat-rate `ollama_cloud` account.
**LOCAL-FIRST / CLOUD-ESCALATION CASCADE (Akien 2026-06-30):** run every tier on free local Hex first; the cloud account is the ESCALATION rung, reserved for the one model too big to run locally (`qwen3-coder-next` 48GB worker) so the highest-end model only burns on genuine need. Optimize use of the high-end model by doing everything else locally.
**Proposed shape:** (1) Register a per-tier `ModelSpec(..., source_name='ollama', ...)` for EACH Hex model above (minion/worker/analyst) in models_registry.py alongside the cloud specs. NOTE: route() pairs rule.model_id with rule.source_name INDEPENDENTLY of the spec's own source_name (rules_engine.py:189-191) — so no model_id collision; you do NOT need to rename the existing cloud devstral spec. (2) Add per-tier `RoutingRule(task_class=<tier>, model=<hex-model>, source='ollama')` in `_DEFAULT_RULES`, with explicit LOCAL-preferred-when-available logic (the candidate sort currently prefers flat_rate over local — must add local-`ollama`-wins-when-`available` or cloud keeps winning); cloud (incl. the 48GB qwen3-coder-next) remains as fallback/escalation. (3) DS drops `model_override` and requests the `worker` TIER (honor the tier-not-model contract — passing an explicit model is the current violation). (4) Set `INFERENCE_ENDPOINT=http://10.0.0.100:11434` in the DS launch env / uu_bash_profile (local `ollama` source still points at 127.0.0.1 today). (5) Reconcile the DEAD source name `local_ollama` in rules_engine.py:95-96 (batch rules) → `ollama`.
**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/models_registry.py, rules_engine.py, devices/dicksimnel/device.py (drop model_override → worker tier); env (uu_bash_profile.sh / DS launch env). Inference Proxy only.
**Design rules:** ALL inference through the Proxy; no direct OR/Anthropic; log the source/model selection at the crossing; no SQLite.
**Scope boundary:** routing + registration + env only; does NOT touch ToolLoop, the trivial ticket, or the smoke driver (sibling tickets).
**Completion criteria:** rules-engine unit test — a `worker` dispatch resolves to source `ollama` @ `http://10.0.0.100:11434` when the local source is available, and to `ollama_cloud` (incl. the 48GB qwen3-coder-next escalation) when Hex is unreachable; same local-first behavior verified for `minion` and `analyst`; grep `local_ollama` → 0 live refs; DS device requests worker tier (no model_override).
**Test plan:** rules_engine selection test red→green per tier (local preferred when available; cloud fallback/escalation when not); grep-gate on `local_ollama`; assert Hex-down → worker falls back to cloud rather than hard-failing.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
